evals.prompt: Extract ISO 29148 compliant requirements from the Python file at /Users/ray/Workspace/uncertainty/skills/sync-req/tools/extract_requirements.py. Create living requirements with bidirectional traceability - each requirement must link to specific code locations (file:line:column), include verification criteria that reference back to the code, and track implementation status.
evals.expected_output: A Markdown file containing ISO 29148 compliant living requirements. Each requirement should have Implementation field with code location, Last Validated date, and Verification criteria that check back to code.

assertions.text: Output is a Markdown file with ISO 29148 structured requirements
assertions.description: The output file should have .md extension and contain ISO 29148 compliant requirements structure

assertions.text: Requirements use 'shall' language per ISO 29148 standard
assertions.description: All requirement descriptions should use 'shall' for mandatory requirements

assertions.text: Each requirement has unique ID in REQ-### format
assertions.description: Requirements should be numbered sequentially as REQ-001, REQ-002, etc.

assertions.text: All requirements include Implementation field with code location
assertions.description: Each requirement must have an Implementation field linking to file:line:column

assertions.text: All requirements include verification criteria
assertions.description: Each requirement must have a Verification field describing how to verify it

assertions.text: Requirements include Status field (Draft, Pending, Implemented, etc.)
assertions.description: Status tracking should be present for each requirement

evals.prompt: Create living requirements for a user authentication system based on this user story: 'As a user, I want to log in with email and password so that I can access my protected resources.' Include code implementation locations, verification criteria, and traceability information following ISO 29148.
evals.expected_output: A Markdown file with ISO 29148 structured living requirements covering authentication workflow, with Implementation, Verification, and Status fields for each requirement.

assertions.text: Output is a Markdown file with ISO 29148 structured requirements
assertions.description: The output file should have .md extension

assertions.text: Requirements use 'shall' language per ISO 29148 standard
assertions.description: All requirement descriptions should use 'shall' for mandatory requirements

assertions.text: Includes functional requirements covering authentication workflow
assertions.description: Requirements should cover login, logout, session management

assertions.text: Each requirement has Implementation field
assertions.description: Implementation field with code location should be present

assertions.text: Each requirement has verification criteria
assertions.description: All requirements must have a Verification field

assertions.text: Each requirement has Status and Last Validated fields
assertions.description: Status tracking metadata should be included

evals.prompt: Generate a DOORS-compatible CSV file with requirements for a REST API that manages user profiles. The CSV should include ID, Text, Type, Priority, Status, Verification, Implementation, Last_Validated, and Rationale columns. Include traceability information linking requirements to code locations.
evals.expected_output: A CSV file with proper DOORS column structure containing user profile management requirements in ISO 29148 format with Implementation and traceability columns.

assertions.text: Output is a CSV file with proper DOORS column structure
assertions.description: CSV should have headers including Implementation, Last_Validated

assertions.text: CSV uses UTF-8 encoding
assertions.description: File should be UTF-8 encoded

assertions.text: Requirements use 'shall' language
assertions.description: Requirement text should use 'shall' for mandatory requirements

assertions.text: Each requirement has unique ID in REQ-### format
assertions.description: Requirements should be numbered sequentially

assertions.text: Each requirement has Verification criteria
assertions.description: Verification field should describe how to verify

assertions.text: Each requirement has Implementation field
assertions.description: Implementation field should contain code location reference

evals.prompt: Extract living requirements from the Python file at /Users/ray/Workspace/uncertainty/skills/sync-req/tools/extract_requirements.py. Save them to a custom output directory named 'custom_docs'. Each requirement must include Implementation field with file:line:column, verification criteria, and status tracking.
evals.expected_output: Requirements extracted and saved to 'custom_docs' directory with a requirements.md file containing ISO 29148 compliant living requirements with traceability.

assertions.text: Output is saved to the specified custom directory 'custom_docs'

assertions.text: Output is a Markdown file with ISO 29148 structured requirements
assertions.description: The output file should have .md extension

assertions.text: Requirements use 'shall' language
assertions.description: All requirement descriptions should use 'shall'

assertions.text: Each requirement has unique ID in REQ-### format
assertions.description: Requirements should be numbered sequentially

assertions.text: All requirements include Implementation field

assertions.text: All requirements include verification criteria
assertions.description: Each requirement must have a Verification field

evals.prompt: Extract living requirements from the Python test file at /tmp/multi_feature_test.py. Split the output into multiple files based on features, each with traceability information. Save to test_output directory. Create an index.md that links to all feature files.
evals.expected_output: Multiple Markdown files organized by feature areas with an index.md file. Each requirement should have Implementation, Verification, and Status fields.

assertions.text: Output directory contains multiple Markdown files
assertions.description: Multiple .md files should be created for different features

assertions.text: An index.md file exists with links to feature files
assertions.description: Main index file should be created with navigation links

assertions.text: Feature files are named appropriately (e.g., *_requirements.md)
assertions.description: Feature files should have descriptive names

assertions.text: Index contains links to feature-specific files
assertions.description: Index should contain markdown links to each feature file

assertions.text: Requirements use 'shall' language
assertions.description: All requirement descriptions should use 'shall'

assertions.text: Each requirement has Implementation field
assertions.description: Implementation field with code location should be present

assertions.text: Each requirement has unique ID in REQ-### format
